title: Kevin Garnett wasn’t a fan of Jayson Tatum missing Game 7 vs. Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Garnett was 'shocked' Jayson Tatum missed Game 7 vs. the Sixers, believing it hurt team morale.
Tatum was ruled out with left knee stiffness before the Celtics' season-ending 109-100 loss to Philadelphia.
Paul Pierce supported the decision to sit Tatum, emphasizing player health over risking further injury.

title: 2 people wounded, 1 seriously, after shooting in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Two people were shot Sunday night on Kingsdale Street in Dorchester, Boston police reported.
A woman sustained serious life-threatening injuries, while a man suffered non-life threatening injuries.
Police are investigating with no arrests made and believe no ongoing public threat exists.

title: NBA insider: Atlanta Hawks were interested in trade for Jaylen Brown last year
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Atlanta Hawks considered acquiring Celtics' Jaylen Brown in a trade last summer, according to NBA insider Chris Mannix.
Talks between the Celtics and Hawks stalled due to Brown's contract size and Atlanta's hesitance to commit.
Jaylen Brown expressed his desire to remain in Boston despite trade rumors and first-round playoff exit frustration.

title: Woman charged in Southborough fatal crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Abigail Throckmorton, 23, charged with motor vehicle homicide in February crash on Turnpike Road, Southborough.
Throckmorton allegedly drove at 70 mph, speeding and driving against traffic, hitting Crystal Johnson-Clementi's car.
Released on personal recognizance, Throckmorton must remain sober, stay in-state, and is prohibited from driving.

title: What’s causing an engine shake in my 10-year-old SUV?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2016 Audi Q5 engine misfire caused shaking and activated the check engine and EPC lights.
Transmission issues in a 2021 Chevy Colorado might be resolved with a fluid service and inspecting for internal wear.
Sparse spare tire kits in some hybrids can complicate flat repairs, impacting tire pressure sensor performance.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox fall flat against Rays on Mother’s Day
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Boston Red Sox lost 4-1 to the Tampa Bay Rays at Fenway Park on Mother's Day, dropping their record to 17-23.
Pitcher Payton Tolle allowed three earned runs over five innings in an emotional start honoring his late mother.
Mickey Gasper, replacing injured Willson Contreras, went 3-for-4 with an RBI and two doubles, showing versatility in multiple positions.

title: Boston gang members, associates facing slew of firearm, drug trafficking charges
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eight defendants linked to Columbia Point Dawgs and Johnston Road gangs face firearm and drug trafficking charges in Boston.
Raugh Williams and others allegedly distributed fentanyl and sold firearms, including illegal machine gun conversion devices.
Federal investigation led to seizure of 18 firearms and large quantities of fentanyl, enhancing community safety, prosecutors state.

title: Photos: Scenes from a fun, but soggy, Saturday in Greater Boston
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: LEGO Group's Duckling Day event held on Boston Common featured duck-themed activities with LEGO® DUPLO® bricks.
Somerville's PorchFest showcased free neighborhood music on porches and stages, drawing thousands despite rainy weather.
Mojo Boston Music Festival offered a 10-hour celebration of contemporary sound and urban art at City Hall Plaza.

title: New Bedford uncle pleads guilty to killing nephew during family altercation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Juan Padilla-Santana, 43, pleaded guilty to manslaughter for the 2024 stabbing death of his 23-year-old nephew, Omil Padilla-Corsino.
Padilla-Santana was sentenced to 12 ½ to 17 ½ years in prison by a Fall River Superior Court judge.
The incident occurred during an argument in a New Bedford apartment, with Padilla-Corsino dying from two stab wounds.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 comeback win against the Philadelphia Union
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Revolution beat Philadelphia Union 2-1, remaining undefeated at home with a 6-0-0 record in 2026.
Luca Langoni and Carles Gil scored crucial goals after a Will Sands own goal initially put New England behind.
New England showed resilience, marking its fourth comeback win in 2026, solidifying second place in the Eastern Conference.
